metadata.method_name: Cross-Path Allen-13 Intersection over Span-LOCAL LLM reads vs Best-Single-Path composition (decisive real-text test)
metadata.description: Tests whether the cross-path full-PC INTERSECTION of disjunctive Allen interval reads narrows deduction-required real-text temporal queries strictly beyond the BEST SINGLE PATH's composition, at matched single-relation coverage with a doc-clustered paired bootstrap (bracketing CI), Holm-adjusted, on the FROZEN NarrativeTime + TDDMan gold graphs. Uses the FULL Allen interval algebra (13 relations), not the iter-3 coarse convex point start-point projection that made full==naive.

config.closure_algebra: FULL Allen interval algebra (13 base relations)

headline_findings.binding_constraint: real-text Allen-read informativeness (constituent edges read as near-universe/underdetermined by BOTH a weak and a strong cross-family reader) -> no composition bite -> mechanism cannot realize on real text
headline_findings.tags: every number tagged REAL-LLM-READ / GOLD-ONLY-GATE / SYNTHETIC-ALLEN-CONTROL / THEOREM

matres_validation.note: MATRES is point-algebra start-point gold (tokens <,=,>; not Allen) and intra/adjacent only -> deduction envelope ~empty. Expected ~0; confirms the gate is discriminative (does not fire on a corpus with no multi-path structure).

precision_recall_impossibility.interpretation: The high-recall disjunctive read (breadth ~11.5/13, recall 0.90) is SOUND but near-universe -> 0 intersection bite. Forcing a single tight Allen relation (raw) is tight but only 3.2% correct -> UNSOUND. Text underdetermines interval endpoints, so no reader operating point gives tight-AND-sound Allen reads; the cross-path coding mechanism therefore cannot realize on real text regardless of prompt or model.

synthetic_allen_control.note: consistent-by-construction Allen QCNs, noisy reads; positive control that the comparison code detects a true intersection>best-single effect when reads are sound.

H_main_intersection_vs_best_single.ci_note: percentile CI brackets the bootstrap MEDIAN by construction; `brackets` reports whether it also contains the full-sample point gap (R1 fix: a CI that excludes its own point estimate is flagged, never reported silently).

H_iter_intersection_vs_naive.ci_note: percentile CI brackets the bootstrap MEDIAN by construction; `brackets` reports whether it also contains the full-sample point gap (R1 fix: a CI that excludes its own point estimate is flagged, never reported silently).

naive_vs_best_single.ci_note: percentile CI brackets the bootstrap MEDIAN by construction; `brackets` reports whether it also contains the full-sample point gap (R1 fix: a CI that excludes its own point estimate is flagged, never reported silently).

H_intersection_vs_pot.ci_note: percentile CI brackets the bootstrap MEDIAN by construction; `brackets` reports whether it also contains the full-sample point gap (R1 fix: a CI that excludes its own point estimate is flagged, never reported silently).

intersection_vs_raw.ci_note: percentile CI brackets the bootstrap MEDIAN by construction; `brackets` reports whether it also contains the full-sample point gap (R1 fix: a CI that excludes its own point estimate is flagged, never reported silently).
